text.title: Reasoning-First: Production Cost Control and Gotchas
text.summary: Cost guards, caching strategy, human-in-loop requirements, and reasoning-specific failure modes in production.

rule.statement: Add per-call cost guards in agent loops: o3 with reasoning_effort=high can cost $5-15 per call. Set a hard budget per reasoning call and abort if the estimated token count exceeds it.
rule.rationale: Reasoning tokens are billed as output tokens. A single reasoning call with a large thinking budget and complex task can consume the monthly budget of a small team. Cost guards prevent runaway spending in automated agent loops.

rule.statement: Cache reasoning outputs for identical inputs using content hash as the key. Reasoning tokens are non-deterministic even at temperature=0 — cache the final answer, not the reasoning trace.
rule.rationale: Reasoning calls are expensive. Identical tasks in agent loops (same planning step, same verification) should not re-invoke the reasoning model. Cache the final answer by input hash; discard the reasoning trace after extracting the answer.

rule.statement: Gate any irreversible action (deploy, delete, send, payment) that follows from reasoning output behind a human review checkpoint. Reasoning models can produce confident-sounding but incorrect reasoning chains.
rule.rationale: Extended thinking is not verified reasoning — it is a prediction of what reasoning would look like. Models hallucinate confidently-sounding logic chains just as they hallucinate facts. Human review prevents automated execution of plausible-but-wrong reasoning.

list.item: Claude Extended Thinking: cannot be combined with system prompt caching in the same request (as of 2026-01); check API changelog
Claude Extended Thinking: cannot be interrupted mid-thought; no streaming of reasoning tokens to the user; set timeout >120s in agent harness
o3/o4 tool use: batch tool calls per reasoning step to reduce round-trips; interleaved thinking with tool calls has higher latency per tool
DeepSeek R1: think blocks may contain PII echoed from input — scrub before logging
o3/o4: reasoning tokens are non-deterministic even at temperature=0; don't diff raw reasoning between runs

antipattern.description: Using reasoning models for high-throughput classification (thousands of calls per minute).
antipattern.reason: Reasoning models have higher latency (5-30s per call) and cost (10-50x). At high throughput, reasoning cost becomes prohibitive and latency makes the system unusable. Use standard models with classification prompts; escalate to reasoning models only for edge cases flagged by the classifier.
